source.quote: ...clearly define the responsibilities of different stakeholders, both within the organisation and with external vendors...

requirements.normativeText: Responsibilities for each phase of the agent lifecycle must be assigned to named roles and captured in the control register.

source.quote: ...emphasising adaptive governance, so that the organisation is set up to quickly understand new developments and update its approach as the technology evolves.

requirements.normativeText: A named governance forum must review agent incidents and emerging risks on a defined cadence and hold authority to adjust permissions.

implementation.note: Ratified v1.0 charter of the GCFO Finance AI Council (GFAIC) -- federated body operating under GAISC Delegation No. 2026-04 from the enterprise-wide Group AI Safety Council (GAISC). Scope bounded to Finance-function agentic AI; binding authority within that scope; cross-function matters escalate to GAISC.

verification.note: GFAIC minutes filed monthly at docs/governance/minutes/YYYY-MM-gfaic.md; redacted copy shared with GAISC within 5 BD.

source.quote: "human-in-the-loop" has to be adapted to address automation bias... defining significant checkpoints in the agentic workflow that require human approval, such as high-stakes or irreversible actions...

requirements.normativeText: The workflow must name every checkpoint where a human must approve an agent action before it proceeds, and the checkpoint definition must cite a high-stakes or irreversible action category.

verification.checklistItem: All DP states have an assigned human owner.

source.quote: ...regularly auditing human oversight to check that it remains effective over time.

requirements.normativeText: Effectiveness of human oversight must be measured and reported periodically (e.g. override rates, review durations) against agreed KPIs.

verification.note: Quarterly KPI pack reviewed by finance leadership.
